claim: LLMs cannot reliably play chess despite being good at explanation; they hallucinate positional understanding due to language training
actionable: Do not use LLMs directly for calculation-heavy tasks; separate calculation engine from explanation layer

claim: Separating concerns: use specialist engines (Stockfish) for calculation, LLMs only for translating structured signals to language
actionable: Decompose agent tasks: specialist systems for computation (chess engines, physics simulators, code execution); LLM for explanation only

claim: Context extraction (forks, pins, positional themes) is critical preprocessing; LLM performance improves dramatically with structured input
actionable: Build domain-specific feature extractors before feeding to LLM; quality of extracted context determines output quality more than model

claim: LLM as translator paradigm: keep LLM focused on language generation, not reasoning; prevents hallucination in constrained domains
actionable: Design LLM prompts to translate, not reason; provide all reasoning via structured data; ground outputs in pre-computed facts

claim: Gemini 3 Flash achieves sub-3-second latency for consumer AI app commentary generation; critical for interactive user experience
actionable: Use Gemini 3 Flash for latency-sensitive applications requiring sub-3s TTFT; benchmark against alternatives for real-time constraints

claim: Automated evals with 16+ test scenarios covering tactical patterns/blunders better than benchmarks for domain-specific quality validation
actionable: Create domain-specific evaluation suites covering critical failure modes; use LLM-as-judge but validate with domain experts

claim: Closing feedback loop via MCP Channels: inject user feedback events into Claude Code sessions for continuous autonomous improvement
actionable: Use MCP Channels to connect user feedback → Claude Code → PR submissions; enables autonomous agent improvement loops

claim: Claude Thinking (extended reasoning) achieves only 60% accuracy on chess scenarios vs Gemini Flash 75%; Thinking slower but not better
actionable: Use Gemini 3 Flash for chess/domain tasks over Claude Thinking if latency matters; Thinking doesn't guarantee quality in constrained domains

claim: Domain experts are essential for eval validation; automated scoring alone misses nuance; SMEs required even for LLM-as-judge evals
actionable: Involve domain experts in evaluation; do not rely purely on LLM-as-judge; expert override critical for quality validation
